label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what can be learned from the text. The options are A, B, C, D. The text talks about Fox Point, a new building in the South Bronx, which is a poor area. They have a clean boiler room that reuses heat to reduce carbon emissions and cut costs. Then it mentions Palladia and Enterprise Community Partners (ECP) with their Green Communities Standards. The text also states that in New York City, almost 80% of greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. The mayor, Michael Bloomberg, wants to create 165,000 affordable units for 500,000 people, and the DHPD will require new projects to follow ECP's standards. Other cities like Cleveland and Denver have similar measures, but New York's DHPD is the largest. Looking at the options: A) New York City is seriously polluted. The text mentions that buildings are responsible for 80% of emissions, but "seriously polluted" might be too strong. The focus is on emissions from buildings, not overall pollution levels. B) People's daily life causes many carbon emissions in New York City. The text says that buildings (including housing) account for 80% of the city's greenhouse gas emissions. So if buildings are part of daily life (like homes and offices), then B might be correct. But the text specifically attributes 80% to buildings, which include both residential and commercial. The 40% of that 80% is from housing. So daily life (residential) is part of the cause, but maybe not the only cause. However, the question is about what can be learned. The text states that a large portion of emissions are from buildings, which are related to daily life (residential and commercial). So B might be correct. C) A great number of people in New York City don't have houses to live in. The text mentions affordable housing projects and that Fox Point is for formerly homeless
